title: Grabowski kill-switch recovery and proof isolation
goal: Prevent test or generic trusted-owner mutations from activating the production operator kill switch, and provide one narrow hash-bound recovery path that preserves fail-closed behavior.
completion: The canonical kill-switch marker cannot be created, replaced, removed or destroyed through generic filesystem or shell surfaces, including trusted-owner mode.
Kill-switch engagement and disarm are exposed only through typed, audited operations with exact marker identity and recovery prerequisites.
A live denial proof uses the deployed artifact in an isolated temporary state root and never changes the canonical production marker.
The current leftover T030 proof marker is removed through a hash-bound reversible break-glass receipt, followed by runtime readback.
Focused and full tests, diff-bound review, deployment and live verification are complete.

root_cause: trusted_owner bypasses protected generic write targets
the production kill-switch marker was created by a generic create operation for a T030 denial proof
the engaged marker blocks every available in-band cleanup surface
the proof had no isolated state root or atomic external cleanup contract
metadata.waiting_reason: T001 is merged, deployed and live-verified. Initiative completion still requires ready task T002 to move canonical marker authority to a root- or dedicated-service-owned boundary that arbitrary same-UID execution cannot bypass.
